commands.review_summary:
Adds cooperative yielding and abort checks while draining Ollama native NDJSON streams, plus a dense-chunk regression test that verifies timer progress before stream completion.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +41. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Partly: source inspection shows current main drains parsed Ollama native stream chunks without a cooperative scheduler, and the PR adds a focused dense-chunk timer-yield regression test, but I did not rerun the original Windows local-daemon reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the maintainer-labeled member PR needs a human landing decision and should not close the linked broader starvation issue by itself.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- ports the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yields and checks aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- adds a regression test that proves timers can run before dense stream processing completes

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate on the maintained branch before the latest clean rebase, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree after the latest rebase, formatter/lint/diff checks, and branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` with 11/11 tests after rebasing to `97ab3fd0d60`; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.78)`. Earlier AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_44ae10129cdd`, slug `golden-prawn`, run `run_565207623b61`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions` and `extensionTests`.

Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. The AWS Crabbox changed gate was not rerun after the latest clean rebase because the patch stayed scoped to the same two Ollama files; this is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
target.author: vincentkoc
